name: Lasko 755320 Ceramic Tower Heater with Remote
tag: Best Ceramic Tower Heater for Living Rooms
introText: The Lasko 755320 is the most consistently top-rated ceramic tower heater on Amazon.ca — oscillating, remote-controlled, with a cool-touch exterior and tip-over protection, at $99 with a years-long reliability track record.
whatWorks: Reliability track record is the defining feature — the Lasko 755320 has been among the top-selling and highest-rated tower heaters on Amazon.ca for years, with thousands of reviews from owners who return to update after 2, 3, and 5+ years. Consistent long-term performance is well documented.

Cool-touch exterior is safe for living rooms with children and pets — even at full 1500W output, the outer housing stays cool to the touch, eliminating the burn risk from exposed ceramic surfaces or metal grilles on traditional space heaters.

Oscillating tower distributes heat across a room — the rotating tower design spreads warmth in a wide arc, more effective than fixed heaters at creating even temperature distribution in living rooms and open spaces.
8-hour timer with auto-shutoff provides fire safety for overnight and unattended use — set it for 2, 4, or 8 hours and it shuts off automatically, reducing risk from heaters left on accidentally.
worthKnowing: 1500W is the maximum power draw for a 120V outlet heater — all standard electric space heaters share this ceiling. The Lasko's oscillation advantage is in heat distribution, not wattage.
Plastic tower exterior is functional but not premium — the build quality matches the price point. Serviceable and durable, but doesn't have the build quality of De'Longhi or Dyson.

Fan noise on high heat is audible — the ceramic element requires a fan to distribute heat, and the fan noise is noticeable in quiet environments. Not suitable for silent bedroom operation (see De'Longhi HMP1500 for that use case).
No precise digital thermostat — the Lasko uses a dial thermostat rather than digital 1° increment control. Acceptable for most users, but less precise than premium models.

commonComplaints.title: Fan noise is noticeable in quiet…
commonComplaints.body: Fan noise is noticeable in quiet environments — the ceramic fan heater produces audible fan sound that can be heard during quiet activities like reading or sleeping.

commonComplaints.title: Dial thermostat less precise than digital…
commonComplaints.body: Dial thermostat less precise than digital alternatives — the analogue temperature dial doesn't allow setting a specific temperature, just a relative position on the dial.

commonComplaints.title: Plastic build feels lightweight compared to…
commonComplaints.body: Plastic build feels lightweight compared to more expensive heaters — the construction is functional but feels less substantial than De'Longhi or Dyson alternatives at 2-4x the price.

verdict: The Lasko 755320 is the best ceramic tower heater for the money, period. Its combination of reliability, safety features, oscillation, and consistent long-term performance is unmatched at $99. The fan noise rules it out for bedroom use, but for living rooms, offices, and shared spaces where heat distribution matters, it's the smart buy.

realBuyers.quote: 5 years of daily winter use and still going. I've replaced the batteries in the remote twice. That's it.
The oscillation makes a huge difference in our living room. The heat doesn't just pool in one spot — the whole room warms up evenly.
Cool-touch exterior is the feature that sold me with young kids. They can touch it without burning themselves.

metaDescription: Lasko 755320 review for Canada: 1500W ceramic, oscillating tower, cool-touch, 8-hr timer, remote. ~$99 CAD on Amazon.ca. Best ceramic tower heater.
